title: Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans XII
body: The love of the brethren at Troas salutes you; whence also I write to you by Burrhus, whom ye sent with me, together with the Ephesians, your brethren, and who has in all things refreshed me. And I would that all may imitate him, as being a pattern of a minister of God. Grace will reward him in all things. I salute your most worthy bishop, and your very venerable presbytery, and your deacons, my fellow-servants, and all of you individually, as well as generally, in the name of Jesus Christ, and in His flesh and blood, in His passion and resurrection, both corporeal and spiritual, in union with God and you. Grace, mercy, peace, and patience, be with you for evermore!

source.label: Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans (shorter recension; trans. Roberts-Donaldson, 1885)

author: engine

witnesses.label: Middle Recension — Codex Mediceus Laurentianus 57.7 (10-11th cent)
Polycarp, Letter to the Philippians — cites Ignatius's letters by name
